steps.sentence: "Each had six wings: with two he covered his face,
and with two he covered his feet,
and with two he flew."
<align="right">- Isaiah 6:2
The hush of rain permeates the air, carrying in a cool breeze.
A pristine glass door stands unassumingly along a narrow corridor, with an aluminium shutter folded neatly at the top.
The interior is obscured by the condensation clouding the glass, yet one could vaguely make out movement occasionally stirring within.
The door opens. A bell above it jingles softly.
A figure steps in, shaking rainwater from their umbrella.
The room is small. Somewhere deeper within, a machine emits a soft, intermittent beep.
A reception counter sits empty at the side, cluttered with items.
What would you like to examine?
A box of name cards sits quaintly at the corner of the desk.
Upon inspection. the words etched on it read: "Dr Angel. Medical Practitioner and Surgeon. Board Certified."
On a metal plaque on the wall, black engravings fill the frame:
“★★★★★ Dr Angel cured my 9-to-5 back pains, she’s a miracle worker!”
“★★★★★ Effective and thoughtful doctor. I can’t stop coming back.”
“★★★★ Dr Angel fixed my wisdom tooth.”
“★★★★★ I cannot believe this level of service is all free!”
A poster hangs on a wall beside a desk.
Above a smiling baby cherub in scrubs, bold words inscribe:
“A doctor’s duty is to make all your pesky maladies disappear!”
Is there anything else you would like to examine?
It seems like the doctor here is well reputed.

steps.speaker: Patient
steps.sentence: Hello? Are you open?
Silence, before…
Yes, come in.
A soft, gentle voice resounds from beyond a door further in that is left ajar.

steps.speaker: Patient
steps.sentence: Are you Dr Angel?

steps.speaker: DR ANGEL
steps.sentence: I am.
The figure hesitates.

steps.speaker: Patient
steps.sentence: I heard this place helps people.

steps.speaker: Dr Angel
steps.sentence: We try our best
Anomaly Report #AN-999 drafted by █████████

AN-999 is a clinic located in the abandoned apartment complex ██████ ███ in ███ ███, located on the ground floor. Preliminary surveys have found that the clinic is locked with shutters on all days of the week except for Sundays.
When the shutters are lifted, there is a glass door leading into a room with white walls, wooden flooring, and a reception counter typical of a family clinic.
There is no trace of the clinic in physical or online advertisements.
Victims who have visited the clinic cannot recall where they heard about the clinic from, while some do not remember visiting it at all, leading researchers to suspect an antimemetic agent at play.
Currently, most victims have not reported any abnormalities, yet many refuse to discuss, or have trouble recalling, the treatment that they have received at the clinic.
A select few reporting anomalous outcomes express an intense, persistent and unexplainable desire to return*. Taking that into account, the anomaly will be temporarily classified as [PENDING].
* Reported individuals have been placed in quarantine.
Researcher notes:
No personnel has attempted to enter the clinic yet. Probably because no one wants to work on a Sunday.
I’ll give it a look. Something about this feels off.
